pkgbootstrap 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

pkgbootstrap

pkgbootstrap creates a new R package with an opinionated setup in a single function call:

  • air formatting (usethis::use_air()), plus panache.toml and jarl.toml configs
  • git, pushed to GitHub
  • a license (MIT by default; pass any usethis::use_*_license() call)
  • an Rmd README
  • codecov test coverage with an opinionated codecov.yml
  • spell checking
  • R-CMD-check and test-coverage GitHub Actions workflows
  • Dependabot updates for GitHub Actions
  • a pkgdown site deployed via GitHub Pages
  • a .pre-commit-config.yaml with panache, air and jarl hooks pinned to their latest release, plus README-freshness and roxygen2 checks, installed with prek if available

Installation

You can install the development version of pkgbootstrap like so:

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("BjarkeHautop/pkgbootstrap")

Example 

library(pkgbootstrap)
bootstrap_pkg("~/GitHub/mynewpkg")

# or with a different license/author
bootstrap_pkg(
  "~/GitHub/mynewpkg",
  author_name = "First Last",
  author_email = "first.last@example.com",
  license = usethis::use_gpl3_license()
)

Creating the GitHub repository requires a GitHub token that usethis can find; see usethis::gh_token_help() if usethis::use_github() fails.
